title: Nageia piresii
intro: Nageia piresii, also known as Pires' nageia, is a species of coniferous tree in the cypress family Cupressaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and other conditions. Bioactive constituents of Nageia piresii include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nageia piresii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Nageia piresii is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae, order Podocarpales, and class Pinopsida. It belongs to the genus Nageia, which includes several species of conifers native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The genus Nageia is further classified under the family Podocarpaceae, which is a diverse group of conifers found in various parts of the world. Nageia piresii is a species endemic to Sumatra, Indonesia. The classification of Nageia piresii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
